task_completion.description: Verify that a new user role named 'Employee' (slug: 'employee') has been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a WordPress roles management screen (e.g., User Role Editor or similar plugin) showing 'Employee' in the list of available roles.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Employee' role has exactly the required capabilities.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a role editing screen for the 'Employee' role. Only the checkboxes for 'read', 'read_private_posts', and 'read_private_pages' should be selected. All other capabilities should be unchecked.

task_completion.description: Verify that the default 'Subscriber' role has been removed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the WordPress roles list or the 'Change role to...' dropdown in the Users screen. The 'Subscriber' role should no longer be visible.

task_completion.description: Verify that the users from the CSV were bulk imported and assigned the 'Employee' role.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the WordPress 'Users' list screen showing a total of 25 users (or 26 if including the admin), with the imported users having the 'Employee' role assigned in the 'Role' column.

task_completion.description: Verify that the imported users have their standard profile fields correctly mapped (First Name, Last Name, Email).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a user profile edit screen (e.g., for 'asmith', 'ewilliams', or 'yrodriguez'). The 'First Name', 'Last Name', and 'Email' fields must match the expected spot check data.

task_completion.description: Verify that the custom user meta key 'department' was created and populated for the users.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the bottom of a user profile edit screen or a custom fields/meta section showing a field named 'department' containing the correct department string (e.g., 'Engineering' for Alice Smith).

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual WordPress application or used valid WP-CLI commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the WordPress admin dashboard, a user import plugin interface, or a terminal with valid WP-CLI commands executing the import, rather than just a text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the provided '~/staff_directory.csv' file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the file path '~/staff_directory.csv' in a file upload dialog, import plugin settings, or terminal command.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not manually edit HTML to fake the user profiles or role screens.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar should show standard WordPress admin URLs (e.g., wp-admin/user-edit.php, wp-admin/users.php) and the interface should look like a genuine WordPress dashboard without signs of browser developer tools being used to alter text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected role slug is 'employee' with exactly three capabilities: 'read', 'read_private_posts', and 'read_private_pages'. The 'subscriber' role must be deleted. Exactly 25 users should be imported. Spot check examples: Alice Smith (asmith@corpnet.local, Engineering), Elena Williams (ewilliams@corpnet.local, Human Resources), Yara Rodriguez (yrodriguez@corpnet.local, Sales).
